name: smart-classifier
description: RFC 0011 self-improving classifier: labels one input into one of the configured LABELS by consulting a distilled model first (talos:core/model predict-batch, lifecycle-gated server-side) and falling back to the LLM only when the model abstains. Emits {label, confidence, _source} — a drop-in classify node — plus an __ml_distill__ envelope for LLM-labeled inputs so the model learns from production. While the model is llm_only/shadow the gate abstains, so behavior is identical to a plain LLM classify node; the LLM-cost reduction turns on automatically once the model earns hybrid/fast_primary. Provision the model/dataset/policy with ml_provision_classifier and reference it here by MODEL_NAME. Requires an actor-bound workflow (the actor resolves tenancy for serving + distillation).

MODEL_NAME.description: The registered ml_models name to consult (from ml_provision_classifier). Resolved under the execution actor's tenancy; served only once the model reaches hybrid/fast_primary.

SYSTEM_PROMPT.description: Classification instructions for the LLM fallback leg. The allowed labels and the JSON response format are appended automatically; you supply the task description (e.g. 'Classify this support email by urgency.').

LABELS.description: The label set (2+). Both the model's and the LLM's answers are validated against it before leaving the node.

MODEL.description: LLM model identifier for the fallback leg.

PROVIDER.description: LLM provider for the fallback leg. 'ollama' (default, Tier-1, local) keeps input on-host.

MAX_TOKENS.description: Max tokens for the LLM classification response (one label is small).
